mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on equity cash flows and eases financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is confirmed when policy rates trend down without a concurrent inflation acceleration.
failure_condition: If 20d forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or the effect disappears when CPIAUCSL inflation is rising, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals a deteriorating labor market and weaker household income growth, which reduces expected corporate revenues and pushes S&P 500 returns lower over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative 20d SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the sign turns positive when PAYEMS growth is stable or rising.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rebound in RSAFS and HOUST indicates strengthening final demand and housing-sensitive spending, which improves near-term earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to outperform single-series signals and do not yield positive 60d SPX returns in at least 55% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in INDPRO while PAYEMS is flat or falling implies productivity-led output gains rather than labor cost pressure, which should support equity margins and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.5% over 2 months and PAYEMS change <= 0
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction does not predict positive 20d returns better than INDPRO alone, or if returns are negative in most episodes where INDPRO rises while PAYEMS is flat or declining.

mechanism_narrative: Very low FEDFUNDS levels support risk assets through a lower discount rate and easier credit conditions, so the S&P 500 should trend higher over the next month when policy remains near the zero lower bound.
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with FEDFUNDS below 0.5% do not have materially higher 20d SPX returns than episodes above 0.5%, or if the sign is negative in a majority of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly improvement in GDPC1 reflects stronger aggregate demand and revenue growth expectations, which should lift equity prices over the following quarter as earnings estimates are revised upward.
failure_condition: Reject if positive quarterly GDPC1 growth does not correspond to positive 60d SPX returns more often than not, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE is high and PAYEMS is contracting, labor income growth weakens and recession risk rises, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 9% and PAYEMS declines for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition fails to produce negative 20d SPX returns in at least 60% of triggered episodes, or if high UNRATE with falling PAYEMS is associated with positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in HOUST signals housing demand stabilization and a coming improvement in consumption and construction activity, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next week before the broader macro data catch up.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST spikes do not precede positive 5d SPX returns in a majority of triggered observations, or if the sign flips negative after controlling for the same-month level of HOUST.
